channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: AI is painful for journalists but healthy for journalism
item.description: As AI takes on a greater role in our media ecosystem, many journalists look at it like a farmer sees an invasive species: as a force that threatens to slowly choke, kill, and replace their work, potentially threatening their livelihood.
item.dc:creator: Pete Pachal, Fast Company

item.title: iPadOS 19: 4 rumored new features, and 2 I’d like to see
item.description: iPadOS 19 is still quite a ways off, but we’ve already heard some key rumors about Apple’s next tablet operating system update – and it’s more than just iOS on the big screen.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Using a smartwatch could be a game-changer for people with diabetes, new research suggests
item.description: New research indicates that wearing a smartwatch can help Type 2 Diabetes patients stick to an exercise routine and manage their condition.
item.dc:creator: stephen.warwick@futurenet.com (Stephen Warwick), TechRadar

item.title: Simon & Schuster wants to be your favorite content creator
item.description: If you were given $100 and five minutes in a bookstore, which titles would you pick? That’s the premise of Simon & Schuster’s upcoming web series, Bookstore Blitz—the publisher’s latest internet-inspired effort to market its authors. And Bookstore Blitz is just the beginning.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: 9 CRM trends for 2025: AI reshapes the customer equation
item.description: AI has the potential to transform every aspect of an enterprise, but nowhere more so than in customer relationship management (CRM), the outward-facing business functions of sales, marketing, and customer service.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Yes, Cristiano Ronaldo is a playable character in Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves, and it makes more sense than you think
item.description: Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves' second open beta goes live as SNK announces Cristiano Ronaldo as a playable character (yes, really).
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How RamaLama helps make AI model testing safer
item.description: Consider this example: An amazing new software tool emerges suddenly, turning technology industry expectations on their heads by delivering unprecedented performance at a fraction of the existing cost.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Tres pasos para preparar sus datos para la IA
item.description: Aunque muchas organizaciones ya han realizado un pequeño número de pruebas de concepto exitosas para demostrar el valor de la IA generativa, ampliar esas pruebas de concepto y aplicar la nueva tecnología a otras partes del negocio nunca funcionará hasta que la producción de datos listos para la IA s
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 11 modi per fallire con l’intelligenza artificiale
item.description: Le aziende stanno investendo molto denaro in strumenti, servizi e strategie che riguardano l’intelligenza artificiale [in inglese]. Purtroppo, però, i grandi esborsi non sempre garantiscono il successo.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: A Venus flytrap wasp? Scientists uncover an ancient insect preserved in amber that snatched its prey
item.description: Scientists have discovered an ancient wasp with a body like a Venus flytrap to seize and snatch its prey.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US robotics companies push for national strategy, including a central office, to compete with China
item.description: American robotics companies are pushing for a national robotics strategy, including a central government robotics office, to promote the industry at a time that Beijing is making intelligent robots a top national priority.
item.dc:creator: Didi Tang And Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge allows newspaper copyright lawsuit against OpenAI to proceed
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge has ruled that The New York Times and other newspapers can proceed with a copyright lawsuit against OpenAI and Microsoft seeking to end the practice of using their stories to train artificial intelligence chatbots. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Teams successful on 52.2% of ball/strike challenges during spring training robot umpire test
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Teams won 52.2% of their ball/strike challenges during the spring training test of robot umpires, and catchers had a markedly better success rate than pitchers.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Microsoft lauds Hyperlight Wasm for WebAssembly workloads
item.description: Microsoft has unveiled Hyperlight Wasm, a virtual machine “micro-guest” that can run WebAssembly component workloads written in a multitude of languages including C and Python. Introduced March 26, Hyperlight Wasm serves as a Rust library crate.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Graham: 'Very sensitive information' included in group chat
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham has acknowledged that "very sensitive information" was discussed among high-level officials via messaging app Signal, but sees the snafu as a "lessons learned" episode.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Rivian spins out startup focused on ‘micromobility’ EVs
item.description: Rivian Automotive Inc. is spinning out a business that will develop small electric vehicles designed for short trips.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: GameStop adopts policy to buy Bitcoin, joining other firms
item.description: GameStop Corp., the struggling video-game retailer that became a favorite of retail traders during the meme stock frenzy in 2021, said its board has approved a plan to add Bitcoin as a treasury reserve asset.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Samsung announces US prices for its 2025 mini-LED TV lineup, and it’s good and bad news
item.description: Samsung has announced US pricing for its new 4K and 8K mini-LED TVs, and prices for the flagship 8K series are largely higher than in 20204.
item.dc:creator: al.griffin@futurenet.com (Al Griffin), TechRadar

item.title: Cyprus to fund desalination plants at hotels to reduce impact of water shortages on tourism
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — Cyprus said Wednesday it plans to subsidize construction of private desalination plants at hotels to ensure the tourism-reliant island nation has enough fresh water too see it through busy summer seasons when the demands of millions of visitors put a severe strain on dwindling
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: AI study buddies are boosting grades to new heights
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ethernet roadmap: AI drives high-speed, efficient Ethernet networks
item.description: At next month’s Optical Fiber Communication Conference and Exhibition (OFC), many of the technologies that are driving current and future Ethernet development will be on full display by the Ethernet Alliance, which is set to unveil its tenth anniversary Ethernet roadmap.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Databricks’ TAO method to allow LLM training with unlabeled data
item.description: Data lakehouse provider Databricks has unveiled a new large language model (LLM) training method, TAO that will allow enterprises to train models without labeling data.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: US adds Chinese tech firms to its export control list, says they sought US knowhow for military use
item.description: The U.S. has added dozens of companies to its export control, including more than 50 based in China that it says sought advanced knowhow in supercomputing, artificial intelligence and quantum technology for military purposes.
item.dc:creator: Elaine Kurtenbach, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The secret to using generative AI effectively
item.description: Do you think generative AI (genAI) sucks? I did. The hype around everything genAI has been over the top and ridiculous for a while now. Especially at the start, most of the tools were flashy, but quickly fell apart if you tried to use them for any serious work purposes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Conservatives embrace Tesla as liberals ditch Elon Musk
item.description: Conservatives are rallying around Tesla while liberals ditch or distance themselves from Elon Musk’s electric vehicles (EVs) in a surprising about-face for a company whose fate is deeply tied to the politics of sustainability and climate issues.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Vibe coding is groovy
item.description: Vibe coding is most definitely having a moment. Don’t feel bad if you haven’t heard of it—the Wikipedia page for it just went up on March 15 of this year.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Researchers in limbo as Columbia bows to Trump’s demands in bid to restore $400M federal funding
item.description: Researchers at Columbia University remain in limbo after the Ivy League institution bowed to the Trump administration’s demands for unprecedented changes in a bid to restore some $400 million in federal funds cut.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Oracle releases ML-optimized GraalVM for JDK 24
item.description: Oracle has released GraalVM for JDK 24, an alternative Java Development Kit tuned to just-released JDK 24 that uses ML (machine learning)-based profile inference to boost peak performance by about 7.9% on average on microservices benchmarks, the company said.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Tesla sold 42.6% less cars in Europe this year. Here’s why
item.description: Tesla’s market share in Europe continued to shrink in February as sales of the all-electric car maker dropped for a second month even as EV registrations overall on the continent grew.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: Coverage of Nvidia GTC 2025 With its legacy of innovation in GPU technology, Nvidia has become a dominant force in the AI market. Nvidia’s partners read like a technology who’s who list – e.g.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Chinese robot's kung fu moves will make your jaw drop
item.description: A humanoid robot developed in China has transformed from a nimble dancer to performing kung fu moves with surprising precision and balance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Attackers probing backdoor flaw in popular Cisco Smart Licensing Utility, warns SANS
item.description: Organizations running Cisco’s Smart Licensing Utility (CSLU) should update their software as soon as possible to fix two serious vulnerabilities, the SANS Technology Institute has urged.
item.dc:creator: Network World
